channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: US Interior Secretary Deb Haaland reflects on tough choices during a historic tenure
item.description: U.S. Interior Secretary Deb Haaland set out four years ago on a historic journey as the first Native American to serve as a cabinet secretary.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden posthumously pardons Black nationalist Marcus Garvey
item.description: President Joe Biden has posthumously pardoned Black nationalist Marcus Garvey, who influenced Malcolm X and other Black civil rights leaders and was convicted of mail fraud in the 1920s.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that’s probably because it has, at least if you’re measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: David Hamilton, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Clothes in the closets and favorite foods in the fridge: Moving day comes to the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most presidents get to move into the White House once. President-elect Donald Trump is doing it twice, and his wife, Melania, says it's a lot easier the second time around. “I know where I will be going. I know the rooms where we will be living.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: TikTok goes dark as US ban takes effect
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Millions of TikTok users in the United States are no longer able to watch videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular app takes effect.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein madam Ghislaine Maxwell's former London townhouse, linked to Prince Andrew photo, for sale
item.description: Jeffrey Epstein accomplice Ghislaine Maxwell's former upscale London townhouse, site of alleged sex crimes, listed for sale with multimillion-dollar price tag.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Civil rights leaders and King family mark MLK Day as a special call to action as Trump takes office
item.description: Events honoring Martin Luther King Jr.’s legacy and advocating for his vision of a just, nonviolent society will occur the same day as Donald Trump's second presidential inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The rise
item.description: The possibility of the U.S. outlawing TikTok kept influencers and users in anxious limbo during the four-plus years that lawmakers and judges debated the fate of the video-sharing app. Now, the moment its fans dreaded is here, but uncertainty over TikTok’s future lingers.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Missouri lawmakers are going after voter-approved abortion rights. Voters will likely reelect them
item.description: Missouri voters last election approved a constitutional amendment that promised to undo the state’s near-total abortion ban.
item.dc:creator: Summer Ballentine, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US cities largely saw a drop in violent crime in 2024, yet many still feel unsafe
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — As U.S. police departments release preliminary or finalized 2024 crime numbers, many are reporting historic declines in homicides and drops in other violent crimes compared to 2023. In many parts of the country, though, those decreases don't match the public perception.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Proposal would ditch BMI for determining obesity
item.description: According to a September 2024 data brief from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), 40.3 percent of U.S. adults were considered obese between August 2021 and August 2023.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Eggs are about to get even more expensive, expert says. Here's how much
item.description: Eggs are nearly 40% more expensive now than they were a year ago, the Labor Department said this week. It's about to get even worse, one expert says.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Most voters in new survey seek toned-down Trump agenda
item.description: Over 60% of Americans polled disagree with Trump's idea to replace thousands of career civil-service workers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Extreme cold forecast for much of US in coming days
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — Another blast of winter weather is expected in parts of the U.S. in the coming days, including bone-chilling wind in the Northern Plains and unusual snow and ice in the Gulf Coast area. Cold weather forecasted for Monday for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justices agree to hear Maryland case on parents' rights and LGBTQ books
item.description: The parents, represented by the Becket Fund for Religious Liberty, sued Montgomery County Public Schools in 2023 after the school district said it would no longer honor opt-out requests or notify families if a book referencing gender or sexuality would be read in class.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: A battery plant fire in California started during a boom for energy storage
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A fire at one of the world’s largest battery plants in Northern California contained tens of thousands of lithium batteries that store power from renewable energy and have become a growing electricity source.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY and NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Durbin seeks information from Patel personnel files at national security agencies
item.description: Editor's note: This story has been updated to correct the committee that Durbin leads. Sen. Dick Durbin (Ill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Biden affirms Equal Rights Amendment is part of Constitution
item.description: President Joe Biden on Friday asserted that the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA), which prohibits discrimination on the basis of sex, is part of the Constitution, arguing it had met the criteria to be added as the 28th amendment.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Senate advances Laken Riley Act, teeing up final vote
item.description: The Senate voted Friday to advance the Laken Riley Act, putting Republicans a step closer to sending the first bill of the 119th Congress to President-elect Trump for his signature next week and giving him an early win on one of his key issues.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, Fox 5

item.title: Top music acts announced for FireAid, a concert to support L.A. wildfire victims
item.description: The biggest names in pop, rock and country are coming together to raise funds for victims of the Palisades and Eaton wildfires and assist with the recovery.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: PHOTOS: 'Home Alone' house in Illinois sells for $250K over asking price
item.description: A famous house featured in the iconic holiday film "Home Alone" sold this week for nearly $300,000 over its asking price.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, Fox 5

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drone collides with paraglider, forcing emergency landing
item.description: A paraglider who took off from Torrey Pines State Reserve in California was forced to make an emergency landing when a drone became entwined in his parachute.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida grandmother who left infant in hot car found not guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A Florida grandmother was acquitted of manslaughter in the death of her 7-month-old granddaughter, but was convicted on a lesser charge.
item.dc:creator: Staci DaSilva, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about RedNote, a TikTok alternative
item.description: TikTok patrons are turning to a new platform in light of the nationwide ban set to shut down the app on Sunday. RedNote, a Chinese short-form video platform, is gaining ground as users look for an alternative to the social media company owned by the China's ByteDance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: David Lynch, director of 'Twin Peaks,' 'Blue Velvet' and more, dies at 78, family says
item.description: David Lynch, the filmmaker and director behind such movies as “Eraserhead,” “Blue Velvet” and the TV series “Twin Peaks,” has died, his family announced in a message posted to his official Facebook page.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: TikTok CEO attending Trump inauguration
item.description: TikTok CEO Shou Zi Chew will attend President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration next week, one day after a potential ban of the video-sharing platform could take effect in the U.S., according to a source familiar with the plans.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Southwest pilot arrested for suspected DUI at Georgia airport
item.description: A Southwest flight was delayed for five hours Wednesday morning due to a pilot being arrested and charged with DUI, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama inmate asks court to block nitrogen gas execution
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Attorneys for an Alabama inmate scheduled to be the fourth person put to death with nitrogen gas on Wednesday asked a federal judge to block the execution, arguing that the first three inmates showed signs of suffering from suffocation as the gas flowed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: For LA water issues, misinformation spreads nearly as fast as the wildfires
item.description: A billionaire couple was accused of withholding water that could help stop Los Angeles' massive wildfires. Democratic leadership was blamed for fire hydrants running dry and for an empty reservoir. Firefighters were criticized for allegedly using “women's handbags" to fight the fires.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US government sues Southwest Airlines, fines Frontier for chronically delayed flights
item.description: “Airlines have a legal obligation to ensure that their flight schedules provide travelers with realistic departure and arrival times," Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles fire captain loses home to Eaton Fire
item.description: A man tasked with protecting the Los Angeles area from raging wildfires now ranks among those who have lost their homes and everything inside.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, Fox 5

item.title: Disabled Los Angeles fire victim escaped flames in wheelchair
item.description: Galen Buckwalter, who is paralyzed from the waist down, said he watched as the glow of the wildfire raced down the San Gabriel Mountains toward their home in the foothill community of Sierra Madre.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Palisades Fire firefighters taking part in first-of-its-kind cancer study
item.description: The study comes after the International Agency for Research on Cancer officially classified firefighting as a carcinogenic profession.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Smith, Fox 5

item.title: FDA bans red dye No. 3 from foods: What to know
item.description: U.S. regulators on Wednesday banned the dye called Red 3 from the nation’s food supply, nearly 35 years after it was barred from cosmetics because of potential cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
